sections.paragraph:
ἀράομαι 1 aor. ἠράσαμην (s. ἀρά; Hom. et al.; LXX, Philo Vi. Mos. 278) lit. ‘pray’, then (since Trag.) w. neg. content to invoke evil on someone, wish evil, imprecate τί ἀράσωμαι σε (for σοι) what evil, then, shall I invoke upon you? GJs 2:3.—Schmidt, Syn. I 181–83. DELG s.v. ἀρά. TW.

aor. aor. = aorist

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Vi. Vi. = Vita, Vitae

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

Trag.

Trag. = writer (s) of tragedy

trag. = tragedy, writer (s) of tragedy

neg. neg. = negative

GJs GJs = Gospel of James (Protevangelium Jacobi), II a.d.—List 1

Schmidt, Syn. Schmidt, Syn. = JS., Synonymik—List 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 128
